gene_description: 5-Hydroxytryptamine Receptor 2A — major postsynaptic serotonin receptor mediating mood, cognition, and perception. Key pharmacological target for SSRIs, atypical antipsychotics, and psychedelic-assisted therapy.

source.title: Gene-Based Association Analysis Suggests Association of HTR2A With Antidepressant Treatment Response in Depressed Patients
source.finding: HTR2A rs7997012 A-allele homozygotes showed significantly better citalopram response in STAR*D (n=1953). Combined GRIK4+HTR2A risk alleles showed 23% reduction in non-response risk.

GG.effect: Homozygous for the G allele. Associated with lower remission rates to citalopram in the STAR*D study. When combined with GRIK4 rs1954787 TT genotype, risk of non-response increases significantly. The variant affects intronic regulation of HTR2A expression and serotonergic signaling dynamics.
GG.recommendation: If SSRI response is suboptimal after 4-6 weeks, consider earlier switch to alternative antidepressant class or augmentation. Evaluate GRIK4 genotype for combined risk assessment. Consider SNRIs or atypical antidepressants as alternatives.

GA.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate likelihood of SSRI response. One copy of the protective A allele partially compensates for the G allele effect.
GA.recommendation: Standard SSRI dosing and monitoring. Allow adequate trial duration before switching.

AG.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate likelihood of SSRI response. One copy of the protective A allele partially compensates for the G allele effect.
AG.recommendation: Standard SSRI dosing and monitoring. Allow adequate trial duration before switching.

AA.effect: Homozygous for the A allele. Best citalopram response profile in STAR*D. When combined with GRIK4 C-allele, remission likelihood is significantly enhanced. Favorable HTR2A expression profile for serotonergic antidepressant response.
AA.recommendation: SSRIs are likely to be effective. Standard dosing per clinical guidelines.

GG.effect: Homozygous for the G allele at the -1438A/G promoter position. Associated with altered HTR2A transcription, though results across studies are inconsistent regarding direction of effect on antidepressant response. In linkage disequilibrium with rs6313 (102T/C).
GG.recommendation: Consider in context of overall pharmacogenomic profile. Standalone predictive value is limited. Standard antidepressant dosing with monitoring.

GA.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate effect on HTR2A promoter activity.

AG.effect: Heterozygous carrier. Intermediate effect on HTR2A promoter activity.

AA.effect: Wild-type promoter. Standard HTR2A expression and typical serotonergic receptor signaling.
